basic.label: Basic (0–25)
basic.meaning: Initial stage. Limited data available, no energy management plan in place.
basic.typicalProfile: Company lacks systematic monitoring or targets.
basic.recommendedNextSteps: Start by collecting full annual energy data and setting a baseline for electricity and heating consumption.

bronze.label: Bronze (26–50)
bronze.meaning: Basic energy tracking started. Partial data or first energy-saving actions implemented.
bronze.typicalProfile: Usually small facilities without a dedicated ESG strategy.
bronze.recommendedNextSteps: Improve data quality, install smart meters, and define a yearly reduction target.

silver.label: Silver (51–75)
silver.meaning: Active management of energy use and emissions. Some renewable energy or efficient systems introduced.
silver.typicalProfile: Typical for mid-size hotels with clear monitoring.
silver.recommendedNextSteps: Identify high-consumption areas, upgrade lighting to LED, and explore partial renewable energy sourcing (e.g. green electricity tariff).

gold.label: Gold (76–90)
gold.meaning: Advanced management with defined goals and verified reduction achievements.
gold.typicalProfile: Facilities implementing ISO 50001 or corporate sustainability plans.
gold.recommendedNextSteps: Set science-based targets, perform energy audits, and optimise heating and cooling systems.

platinum.label: Platinum (91–100)
platinum.typicalProfile: Facilities powered fully by renewables and with continuous improvement verified by third parties.
platinum.recommendedNextSteps: Maintain certification (ISO 50001/SBTi) and share best practices with industry peers.
